rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FAYEON DISTRIBUTORS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Goldstar Supermarkets Discovery Responses And Deem Admitted All Matters In Plaintiffs Requests For Admission
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 13, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH Motion To Compel Goldstar Supermarkets Discovery Responses And Deem Admitted All Matters In Plaintiffs Requests For Admission. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. No opposition filed. Goldstar Supermarkets shall respond to all pending written discovery requests and shall produce all documents responsive to Plaintiff's First Request for Production within 15 days. Goldstar is deemed to have admitted the truth of the matters specified in Plaintiff's 1st and 2d Requests for Admission. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $3,675. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 13, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT JOHN REAMPULLA Motion For Discovery Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BAKER VS. DIGNITY HEALTH, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Christopher Baker'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants And For Monetary And Other Sanctions (Continued From Aug-11-14)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 13, 2014, Line 5, Plaintiff Christopher Baker's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants And For Monetary And Other Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel Gloria Lau's deposition on August 15, 2014 at 10:00 a.m. is granted. The motion to compel the PMK depositions of Saint Francis Memorial Hospital and Dignity Health are granted only to the extent that such depositions will not be duplicative of Ms. Lau's deposition. The request to shift costs to Defendants for expediting deposition transcripts is denied. Plaintiff's notice of intent to audiotape or videotape the depositions of St. Francis Hospital and Dignity Health is deemed to apply to the deposition of Ms. Lau. Plaintiff may use such tape(s) in preparation of his opposition to summary judgment with the understanding that the reporter's transcript may be filed at a later date to confirm the content of any recorded deposition testimony. The motion to deny Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment is denied without prejudice. The discovery department does not have jurisdiction to decide that motion. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $8,500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NHAN THI PHAN VS. MICHAEL DANG, DBA AS BLUE SKY SPA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 13, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF NHAN PHAN's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. Granted. Plaintiff may file her proposed Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") within 10 days of entry of this Order. Courts are bound to apply a great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint absent prejudice to the adverse party. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) Defendants have not established that they would be prejudiced by the filing of the proposed TAC. No trial date has been set and the factual allegations remain largely the same between the Second Amended Complaint and the proposed TAC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALMA ARCINIEGAS VS. CORE CARE CENTER LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 13, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTS KANE IR (BUSINESS ASSOCIATION, FORM UNKNOWN) and ROBERT KANE, DC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff must comply with the provisions of CCP sec. 425.13 before stating a claim for punitive damages against Defendant Robert Kane DC because her claim "arises out of the professional negligence of a health care provider." (Central Pathology Service Med. Clinic Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 3 Cal.4th 181; Davis v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 623.) The motion is granted without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has previously been given the opportunity to amend her complaint to state a claim for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FLYNN & ENSLOW INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOHN B. ENSLOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 13, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT JOHN ENSLOW MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion is granted. Plaintiff does not oppose the motion to set aside default. Defendant may file and serve the answer submitted with the motion. Plaintiff's request for $1554.95 in fees is denied. The evidence in the Declaration of John B. Enslow in Support of Motion for Relief from Default, filed July 14, 2014 ("Enslow Decl.") establishes that defendant Enslow, in the midst of family wedding preparations, misplaced the summons and complaint, and did not find it until three days before the response to the complaint was due. Defendant, representing himself at that point, tried without success to fax file a response to the complaint from out of state. On March 10, 2014, the next business day after the response was due to have been filed, defendant, representing himself, sent an email to Plaintiff's counsel, explained the circumstances, and asked for an extension of time to respond. (Enslow Decl., para. 10-11, and Ex. A.) Plaintiff's counsel forwarded the email to his client (Defendant's stepsister) that same day. (Id., para. 12 and Ex. B.) On March 11, 2014, Plaintiff's counsel had defendant's default entered without ever having responded to Defendant Enslow's email from the preceding day. Contrary to Plaintiff's assertion in the "Sur-reply in Support of Plaintiff's Non-Opposition to Defendant's Motion for Relief from Default," filed August 5, 2014, Plaintiff was not required by Rule of Court 3.110(g) to file its request for default at that time. (Rule of Court 3.110(g) states in part: "If a responsive pleading is not served within the time limits specified in this rule and no extension of time has been granted, the plaintiff must file a request for entry of default within 10 days after the time for service has elapsed.")(emphasis added) Under the circumstances, an award of fees and costs is not warranted under CCP 473(c)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
